definition:
ἐν-έχω 
 [in LXX: Gen.49:23 (סָתַם), Eze.14:4, 7, 3Ma.6:10 * ;] 
  1. to hold in; pass., to be held, entangled: with dative of thing(s); jig., ζυγῷ δουλείας, Gal.5:1 (cf. MM, Exp., xii); θλίψρεσιν, 2Th.1:4 (cf. ἀσεβείσιν, 3Mac, l.with). 
   2. to set oneself against, be urgent against (as Ge, l.with; for con­struction, see Swete, Mk., l.with): Mrk.6:19, Luk.11:53.†
 (AS)
